item.title: Tommy explains how AI workloads could split between open source and frontier models. “My mental model is like 10% of requests should go to a frontier model, 90% should go to open source.”

item.title: Agents as Webs of Beliefs
item.description: pspanIn this post I’ll sketch out an informal model of intelligent agents as webs of beliefs (or belief webs for short). The belief webs framework pulls together ideas from active inference, agent foundations and machine learning. In doing so it aims to unify beliefs, goals and actions as three face

item.title: Neuralese is Actually Probably Good for Alignment
item.description: pThe best language models are still getting smarter and more capable. To an increasing degree, this is because they are trained by Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards. Chain of thought reasoning allows models to evade the finite depth restriction on information flow by passing (relatively

item.title: ClosedAI unironically naming their models Sol, Terra, and Luna is mostly just a reflection of how completely irrelevant crypto has faded within the tech world.

item.title: Why are adversaries assumed to be incapable of responding to AI risk?
item.description: pWhen I talk to people about what might be done about AI threatening approximately everything that everyone cares about, I notice a common oddity in their resistance to a variety of ideas. They seem to take for granted that certain entities—especially Trump and China—would be acting against their ow

item.title: Tokenized Asset Value Stalls Even as Stock Token Holders Surge
item.description: Distributed tokenized real-world asset value slipped about 1.4% over the past month to roughly $31.5 billion, the first monthly decline of its institutional-led run, even as the number of holders climbed 14%, led by tokenized stocks, according to data from rwa.xyz.

item.title: AI Decline? Confidence in Autonomous Penetration Testing Falls
item.description: Companies are still experimenting with automated AI systems to find security weaknesses, but fewer are relying on the technology.

item.title: Sandy Kory says AI won’t change what great founders look like. “What’s going to stay the same is these certain types of founders: exceptional, outlier, brilliant, determined, resourceful, mission-orie
item.description: pSandy Kory says AI won’t change what great founders look like.br /br /“What’s going to stay the same is these certain types of founders: exceptional, outlier, brilliant, determined, resourceful, mission-oriented, intellectually honest founders.”/pa href="https://nitter.net/Delphi_Digital/status/207

item.title: AI Won't Wipe-Out Entry-Level Cybersecurity Jobs
item.description: Instead of eliminating jobs for early-career cyber pros, AI is creating new opportunities for candidates with strong human decision-making skills.

item.title: BitGo Cuts Nearly 15% of Staff Six Months After IPO, Refocuses on Stablecoins and AI
item.description: Crypto custodian BitGo is cutting approximately 15% of its workforce, CEO Mike Belshe announced Thursday, as the company refocuses on stablecoins, settlement, and AI infrastructure following a difficult post-IPO stretch.

item.title: Jose explains why frontier AI may keep its pricing power. “I think the frontier is always going to be way more expensive and create way more of the economic value.”
item.description: pJose explains why frontier AI may keep its pricing power.br /br /“I think the frontier is always going to be way more expensive and create way more of the economic value.”/pa href="https://nitter.net/Delphi_Digital/status/2070526449111769369#m"br /Videobr / img src="https://nitter.net/pic/amplify_

item.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
item.description: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol, a next-generation model with stronger capabilities in coding, science, and cybersecurity, paired with its most advanced safety stack.

item.title: AI and Liability
item.description: pEarlier this month, a German court a href="https://the-decoder.com/landmark-german-ruling-declares-googles-ai-overviews-are-googles-own-words-and-makes-it-liable-for-false-answers/"ruled/a that Google is liable for its AI search summaries. Rejecting defenses like &#8220;users can check for themselv
